title: SysDa fluent query API: programmatic select without a query string
instruction: In the sandbox model, create a class DemoSysDaReader that reads data through the SysDa fluent API instead of an X++ select. Ground it first with get_knowledge(topic="sysda"). The public static method `container blockedCustomerAccounts()` must build a SysDaQueryObject over CustTable, add a where expression restricting Blocked to CustVendorBlocked::No, project only the AccountNum field, execute it through a SysDaSearchObject / SysDaQueryable execution path, iterate the result with next(), and return the accounts as a packed container. Confirm every SysDa class and member signature with search/get_method before writing it - do not guess a fluent method name. A plain `while select` FAILS this case: the point is to prove the SysDa path compiles and runs. The class must build with zero BP errors.
